lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਚਰੁ ਵਿਚਿ ਦੰਮੁ ਹੈ ਤਿਚਰੁ ਨ ਚੇਤਈ ਕਿ ਕਰੇਗੁ ਅਗੈ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jichar vich dam hai tichar na chetee ki kareg agai jaae |
lines.english: As long as there is breath in the body, he does not remember the Lord; what will he do in the world hereafter?

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਿਆਨੀ ਹੋਇ ਸੁ ਚੇਤੰਨੁ ਹੋਇ ਅਗਿਆਨੀ ਅੰਧੁ ਕਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: giaanee hoe su chetan hoe agiaanee andh kamaae |
lines.english: One who remembers the Lord is a spiritual teacher; the ignorant one acts blindly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਏਥੈ ਕਮਾਵੈ ਸੋ ਮਿਲੈ ਅਗੈ ਪਾਏ ਜਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak ethai kamaavai so milai agai paae jaae |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, whatever one does in this world, determines what he shall receive in the world hereafter. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਧੁਰਿ ਖਸਮੈ ਕਾ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਪਇਆ ਵਿਣੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਚੇਤਿਆ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: dhur khasamai kaa hukam peaa vin satigur chetiaa na jaae |
lines.english: From the very beginning, it has been the Will of the Lord Master, that He cannot be remembered without the True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਰਵਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਸਦਾ ਰਹਿਆ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: satigur miliai antar rav rahiaa sadaa rahiaa liv laae |
lines.english: Meeting the True Guru, he realizes that the Lord is permeating and pervading deep within him; he remains forever absorbed in the Lord's Love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਕਾ ਭਉ ਗਇਆ ਜੀਵਨ ਪਦਵੀ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: janam maran kaa bhau geaa jeevan padavee paae |
lines.english: His fears of birth and death depart, and he obtains the honored state of eternal life.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਮਿ ਦਮਿ ਸਦਾ ਸਮਾਲਦਾ ਦੰਮੁ ਨ ਬਿਰਥਾ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: dam dam sadaa samaaladaa dam na birathaa jaae |
lines.english: With each and every breath, he constantly remembers the Lord in meditation; not a single breath passes in vain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਇਹੁ ਮਰਤਬਾ ਤਿਸ ਨੋ ਦੇਇ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰੇ ਰਜਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak ihu maratabaa tis no dee jis no kirapaa kare rajaae |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, He bestows this rank upon that mortal, upon whom He showers His Mercy. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਰੂਪ ਦਿਖਾਲਦਾ ਆਪੇ ਲਾਇ ਧਿਆਨਾਂ ॥
lines.translit: aape roop dikhaaladaa aape laae dhiaanaan |
lines.english: He Himself reveals His form, and He Himself enjoins us to His meditation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਮੋਨੀ ਵਰਤਦਾ ਆਪੇ ਕਥੈ ਗਿਆਨਾਂ ॥
lines.translit: aape monee varatadaa aape kathai giaanaan |
lines.english: He Himself poses as a silent sage, and He Himself speaks spiritual wisdom.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਦਾਨਾਂ ਬੀਨਿਆ ਆਪੇ ਪਰਧਾਨਾਂ ॥
lines.translit: aape daanaan beeniaa aape paradhaanaan |
lines.english: He Himself is all-wise and all-knowing; He Himself is supreme.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਉੜਾ ਕਿਸੈ ਨ ਲਗਈ ਸਭਨਾ ਹੀ ਭਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: kaurraa kisai na lagee sabhanaa hee bhaanaa |
lines.english: He does not seem bitter to anyone; He is pleasing to all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਸਤਤਿ ਬਰਨਿ ਨ ਸਕੀਐ ਸਦ ਸਦ ਕੁਰਬਾਨਾ ॥੧੯॥
lines.translit: ausatat baran na sakeeai sad sad kurabaanaa |19|
lines.english: His Praises cannot be described; forever and ever, I am a sacrifice to Him. ||19||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਤੁ ਜਿਨੂਰਾ ਧੀਅ ਜਿੰਨੂਰੀ ਜੋਰੂ ਜਿੰਨਾ ਦਾ ਸਿਕਦਾਰੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: put jinooraa dheea jinooree joroo jinaa daa sikadaar |1|
lines.english: The son is a demon, and the daughter is a demon; the wife is the chief of the demons. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਲੀ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਜਿੰਨਾਂ ਦਾ ਅਉਤਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: kalee andar naanakaa jinaan daa aautaar |
lines.english: In this Dark Age of Kali Yuga, O Nanak, the demons have taken birth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਥਰੁ ਲੇ ਪੂਜਹਿ ਮੁਗਧ ਗਵਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: paathar le poojeh mugadh gavaar |
lines.english: The ignorant fools pick up stones and worship them.

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਰਦਿ ਕਹਿਆ ਸਿ ਪੂਜ ਕਰਾਂਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: naarad kahiaa si pooj karaanhee |
lines.english: As Naarad instructed them, they are worshipping idols.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਿੰਦੂ ਮੂਲੇ ਭੂਲੇ ਅਖੁਟੀ ਜਾਂਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: hindoo moole bhoole akhuttee jaanhee |
lines.english: The Hindus have forgotten the Primal Lord; they are going the wrong way.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧੇ ਗੁੰਗੇ ਅੰਧ ਅੰਧਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: andhe gunge andh andhaar |
lines.english: They are blind and mute, the blindest of the blind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਹਿ ਜਾ ਆਪਿ ਡੁਬੇ ਤੁਮ ਕਹਾ ਤਰਣਹਾਰੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: ohi jaa aap ddube tum kahaa taranahaar |2|
lines.english: But when those stones themselves sink, who will carry you across? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਕਿਹੁ ਤੇਰੈ ਵਸਿ ਹੈ ਤੂ ਸਚਾ ਸਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: sabh kihu terai vas hai too sachaa saahu |
lines.english: Everything is in Your power; You are the True King.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਗਤ ਰਤੇ ਰੰਗਿ ਏਕ ਕੈ ਪੂਰਾ ਵੇਸਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhagat rate rang ek kai pooraa vesaahu |
lines.english: The devotees are imbued with the Love of the One Lord; they have perfect faith in Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਭੋਜਨੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਹਰਿ ਰਜਿ ਰਜਿ ਜਨ ਖਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: amrit bhojan naam har raj raj jan khaahu |
lines.english: The Name of the Lord is the ambrosial food; His humble servants eat their fill.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭਿ ਪਦਾਰਥ ਪਾਈਅਨਿ ਸਿਮਰਣੁ ਸਚੁ ਲਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: sabh padaarath paaeean simaran sach laahu |
lines.english: All treasures are obtained - meditative remembrance on the Lord is the true profit.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਤ ਪਿਆਰੇ ਪਾਰਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਅਗਮ ਅਗਾਹੁ ॥੨੦॥
lines.translit: sant piaare paarabraham naanak har agam agaahu |20|
lines.english: The Saints are very dear to the Supreme Lord God, O Nanak; the Lord is unapproachable and unfathomable. ||20||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਕਿਛੁ ਹੁਕਮੇ ਆਵਦਾ ਸਭੁ ਕਿਛੁ ਹੁਕਮੇ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: sabh kichh hukame aavadaa sabh kichh hukame jaae |
lines.english: Everything comes by the Lord's Will, and everything goes by the Lord's Will.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਕੋ ਮੂਰਖੁ ਆਪਹੁ ਜਾਣੈ ਅੰਧਾ ਅੰਧੁ ਕਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: je ko moorakh aapahu jaanai andhaa andh kamaae |
lines.english: If some fool believes that he is the creator, he is blind, and acts in blindness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਕੋ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬੁਝੈ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰੇ ਰਜਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak hukam ko guramukh bujhai jis no kirapaa kare rajaae |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukh understands the Hukam of the Lord's Command; the Lord showers His Mercy upon him. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਜੋਗੀ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਸੋ ਪਾਏ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਪਰਾਪਤਿ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: so jogee jugat so paae jis no guramukh naam paraapat hoe |
lines.english: He alone is a Yogi, and he alone finds the Way, who, as Gurmukh, obtains the Naam.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਜੋਗੀ ਕੀ ਨਗਰੀ ਸਭੁ ਕੋ ਵਸੈ ਭੇਖੀ ਜੋਗੁ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: tis jogee kee nagaree sabh ko vasai bhekhee jog na hoe |
lines.english: In the body-village of that Yogi are all blessings; this Yoga is not obtained by outward show.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਐਸਾ ਵਿਰਲਾ ਕੋ ਜੋਗੀ ਜਿਸੁ ਘਟਿ ਪਰਗਟੁ ਹੋਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak aisaa viralaa ko jogee jis ghatt paragatt hoe |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, such a Yogi is very rare; the Lord is manifest in his heart. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਸੂਖਮੁ ਭਾਲੀਐ ਆਪੇ ਪਾਸਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: aape sookham bhaaleeai aape paasaar |
lines.english: He Himself is seen to be subtle, and He Himself is obvious.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਜੰਤ ਉਪਾਇਅਨੁ ਆਪੇ ਆਧਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: aape jant upaaeian aape aadhaar |
lines.english: He Himself created the creatures, and He Himself supports them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਇਕਾਤੀ ਹੋਇ ਰਹੈ ਆਪੇ ਵਡ ਪਰਵਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: aap ikaatee hoe rahai aape vadd paravaar |
lines.english: He Himself remains a solitary recluse, and He Himself has a huge family.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਰੁ ਦਾਤਾਰੁ ਨ ਸੁਝਈ ਤੂ ਦੇਵਣਹਾਰੁ ॥੨੧॥੧॥ ਸੁਧੁ ॥
lines.translit: hor daataar na sujhee too devanahaar |21|1| sudh |
lines.english: I cannot see any other Giver; You alone are the Giver, O Lord. ||21||1|| Sudh||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕੁ ਮੰਗੈ ਦਾਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਸੰਤਾ ਰੇਨਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: naanak mangai daan har santaa renaar |
lines.english: Nanak asks for the gift of the dust of the feet of the Saints of the Lord.
